entry.title: Re: Introducing Support for Different Time Granularities in Remote Models with Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
@MoonJun , Thanks for the great writeup. Indeed this makes the LINK function more flexible to lookup. Just one clarification on the point 

  • Advanced Formulas can reference time periods with higher or lower granularity than the default model.

In case where the remote model or lookup model has time dimension of higher granularity say month and the default model has lower granularity lets say week, will the PERIOD() function still work as it would be 1:N , It works fine from lower to higher as its N:1 ( multiple lower time frame ( weeks) to 1 higher time frame (month)

Cheers

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Issues in J62 Asset Accounting – Step 4.1.2 “Create P...
entry.content:
Good day @Wayne10 

Thank you for your question.

Based on the behaviour you described, the errors you are facing are most likely not coming from the standard J62 test script but from a specific control or validation logic in your system.

The Public Cloud only allows you to change field properties for purchase requisitions through BAdIs or Custom Logic (for example PR field control BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE) and not via SSCUI. This scenario is addressed in the following: KBA 3268822 and KBA 3345421. The PR check extensibility for custom validation and how to handle error in procurement scenarios is visible in the following reference.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/044700714fc54863b7fa9ea0b5654ba0.html?locale=en-US

So, in your case, this means that a standard J62 free text PR with Asset category assignment account should normally not required Material or Fixed Vendor unless your tenant has an additional rule. Therefore, these mandatory checks usually come from one of the following:

  1. Custom Logic BAdI for PR field control which then makes Material and/or Supplier mandatory. See KBA 3268822 and KBA 3345421 for more details.
  2. Custom PR validation logic that raises hard errors before you save or check it
  3. Source determination or sourcing setup expectation (though this is less likely for a free text J62 PR, but you may consider checking if your process is customised).

The following references provide more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/044700714fc54863b7fa9ea0b5654ba0.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/49fb9b57aba49f2de10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

To narrow down on the source of the issue, the recommendation is:

  • Reproduce the issue in your PR app.
  • Open Custom Logic and check active implementation in the PR business context, especially BAdI BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE. See KBA 3268822 and KBA 3345421 for more details.
  • Review whether any implementation sets Material or Supplier/Fixed Vendor as mandatory for all PR items.
  • Review your PR validation logic for hard error messages that the system triggers if material or vendor is missing.
  • Only if your business process really requires source determination, you may then consider reviewing Manage Source Lists and the relating sourcing setup in your organisation’s procurement process.

You can resolve the issues after those checks by adjusting the custom logic so the mandatory checks become conditional, for example, you can then exclude:

  • Account Assignment Category A
  • Free text PR item
  • The J62 process variant.

That will help you preserve your business controls for other PR scenarios while allowing you to run the standard J62 test step as is intended.

To summarise:

  • The errors you provided in your screenshots are most likely a specific validation related and not standard J62 behaviour.
  • In Public Cloud, PR field control is usually done via Custom Logic BADIs not SSCUI and can make fields mandatory
  • Check you Custom Logic first, then source related setup if you still require it.
  • You can fix the issue by making your system validation conditional for Asset free text PR scenarios.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Achieve Event Driven Architecture and FIFO Approach with Google PubSub Adapter in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
With the growing popularity of Event Drive Architecture (EDA), message based adapters are a popular choice. Google PubSub Adapter for SAP Integration Suite is one such adapter. Understanding the architecture of the Google PubSub Subscription is a complex undertaking, therefore this blog attempts to simplify the same by creating a simple Integration Flow (iflow) to achieve Event Driven Architecture with FIFO approach.

Additionally majority of EDA based use cases revolve around the First In First Out (FIFO) Approach. Take E-Commerce for example. One would want that unless an order has been successfully processed there should not be any inventory change or the status of an order should not change to Shipped unless the payment has been completed. Such a crucial mechanism needs to be handled without any chance of error. Using Google PubSub with the necessary configurations, we can create such a scenario with ease.

Google PubSub Concepts

Before getting into the Integration flow, let's talk a little about the architecture of Google PubSub(GPS) itself.

GPS has three main components, a topic, the subscription attached to the topic, and the message that flows from topic to subscription. When a message is published to a topic, a messageId and publishTime is attached to it. Uniqueness of a message is decided by the assigned messageId.

Configuring Google PubSub Topic

Create a new topic and give it a unique ID. You could choose to have a default subscription attached to it. The ID of the default subscription would be decided by GPS itself (usually appending "-sub" after the Topic ID). Keep the rest of the configurations unchecked as they are not relevant to our use case.

Configuring Google PubSub Subscription

You can create a new subscription or work with the default subscription attached to the topic.

If you create a new subscription ensure that it is of Pull delivery type. This ensure subscribers to request for message using the Google PubSub Sender Adapter.

A Pull delivery type subscription has the following configurations.

The important configurations to create an FIFO Event Driven architecture are:

  • Exactly once delivery: Enable to ensure messages are not resent before the Acknowledgement Deadline expires.
  • Message ordering: Enable to ensure that messages with the same ordering key are received by the subscription in the order they were published.
  • Acknowledgement deadline (optional): Decides in how much time an acknowledgement is expected. If no acknowledgement is received within the expiration of this deadline, GPS topic is free to send the message to any of its subscriptions. You must set an acknowledgement deadline greater than the maximum integration flow processing time. This field technically does not affect the FIFO approach but setting a higher value is advantageous in terms of redelivery attempts.

Configuring Google PubSub Sender (REST)

Below is a basic iflow to read messages from a GPS Subscription. An exception subprocess block has been added to handle any error that occurs.

Ensure that the exception sub process ends with an Error End Event.

This is extremely important. The behavior of the Integration Suite and the adapter itself depends highly on these events. Error End Event tells the adapter that the business process flow encountered an error and a negative Message Processing Log (MPL) is created. This negative MPL is dealt with differently as compared to a positive MPL.

After configuring the Connection tab of the GPS Sender (for more info, see SAP Cloud Integration – Getting started with Google Pub/Sub Adapter), switch to Processing tab.

Project ID : ID assigned to your Google Cloud Console project.

Subscription ID: ID of the subscription from which messages will be read.

Max Messages to Read: Maximum number of messages to be read in a single poll. 

More details for these values which can be found here SAP Help.

Select Post-Processing as Acknowledge after Successful Processing; this ensures that the message is not acknowledged by the adapter, if the iflow processing ends with an error i.e. a negative MPL was created. Remember our previous explanation concerning Error End Event.

Following these recommendations and values will ensure an iflow with FIFO approach is successfully designed.

Sample Use Case 1 : Messages with same Ordering Key

The flow is as follows:

  1. Messages M1 and M2 are published to a GPS Topic with the same Ordering key. You can achieve this in a different iflow using the Google PubSub Receiver Adapter or via an external publisher system.
  2. The first message M1 is read by the GPS Sender attached to our iflow. Note that since M1 and M2 were part of the same ordering key and the subscription had Ordering Enabled, GPS does not allow M2 to be even sent before M1 is out of the Topic.
  3. The GPS Sender sends the message M1 to the iflow for processing.
  4. Lets say an error occurs while processing this message M1. The Exception Sub-Process is invoked, and after the necessary steps (as per your business logic; e.g.. create a JIRA ticket), iflow ends with an Error End Event.
  5. A negative MPL is created for the processing of M1. GPS Sender receives this negative MPL back and does not send an acknowledgement to the GPS Subscription.
  6. GPS Subscription does not receive an Acknowledgement for M1 within the set acknowledgement deadline. M1 remains in the topic. 
  7. Google PubSub Sender will not receive the second message M2 until M1 is removed from its topic either by acknowledging it or by moving it to a Dead Letter Queue using a different configuration or by manually removing it.

Sample Use Case 2: Messages with different Ordering Keys

Let us see a similar thing with two messages of same ordering key and another message with a different ordering key.

Messages M1 and M2 published with same ordering key 1

      

Message M3 with a different ordering key 3 published.

Iflow receives M1 and M3 repeatedly until they are successfully processed by the iflow. M2 was not received as its predecessor M1 was not processed completely and was not removed from the topic.

  

 

Enabled Ordering Key in the Subscription ensures that M1 is processed before M2 as they have the same ordering key and M1 was published before M2.

Enabled Exactly Once Delivery ensure M1 is not resent until Acknowledgement Deadline expiration occurs which in this case was 120 seconds.

Conclusion

By leveraging the Google PubSub Adapter in SAP Integration Suite we are able to achieve strict message ordering which is a must if you are focusing on the integrity of an Event Driven Business Model. You can avoid a scenario where "Update" event comes before "Create" event in a database call.

In short Event Driven Architecture means your system stays updated in real-time, FIFO ensures those updates happen in the correct sequence, and Google PubSub Adapter ensures Event Driven Architecture is implemented with FIFO.

Happy Integrating!

entry.title: Part 1: Creating and Deploying a SAP CAP Application with SAP HANA on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry)
entry.content:
Note: This blog is the First Part of series - Building and Deploying SAP CAP Applications on BTP: From Database to Work Zone

 

Introduction

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) is often demonstrated through local examples, but its real value becomes clear when the application is deployed to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and backed by a managed database.

In this blog, we will go through creating a basic SAP CAP application using the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) CAP template, binding it to SAP HANA Cloud, and deploying it to SAP BTP using the Cloud Foundry runtime.

The goal here is not to build a complex application, but to establish a clean, working reference point. I start with a minimal CAP project to keep the focus on deployment rather than domain complexity.

This blog serves as a baseline for a series where the same CAP application will later be deployed using different databases and runtimes. The focus here is not application complexity, but establishing a clean, working reference point.

 

Pre-requisites:

Please make sure that you have these before starting the development:

  • SAP BTP Account
  • Cloud Foundry instance
  • Active Hana Cloud Instance
  • SAP BAS / VSCode for development
  • If using BAS, have a dev space running
  • If using VSCode then make sure that you have installed Node.js and CLI installed

 

Now, let’s start with the development.

 

Create a CAP Project from Template given in the BAS:

SAP Business Application Studio provides a template for creating a CAP project with the required structure. The positives of using the template is that it automatically builds your Multi-Tenant Application file (mta.yaml) with the required deployment modules.

 

Follow the following steps:

  • Open BAS
  • Click on the Side Menu on the top-left corner
  • Click on File
  • Click on New Project From Template
  • Select CAP Project
  • Configure the project as follows:
    • Project Name: cap-hana-app
    • Runtime: Node.js
    • Database: SAP HANA Cloud
    • Deployment Environment: Cloud Foundry
    • Runtime Capabilities: XSUAA
  • Click on Finish

 

CAP Project creation wizard with SAP HANA selected

 

This will create a CAP project for you with mta.yaml automatically updated. The generated project follows standard CAP structure:

  • app –  UI
  • db – Schema and data files
  • srv – Service Definition

 

Now that the project is created, let’s get into development.

 

Development:

Follow the following steps:

  • Define a Simple Data Model:

If you have schema.cds under db/ folder, then update it with the following code snippet. If you do not have schema.cds, then create one.

namespace com.mycompany.capapp;
entity Products {
    key ID          : UUID;
        name        : String(100);
        description : String(500);
        price       : Decimal(10, 2);
        stock       : Integer;
        category    : String(50);
        createdAt   : Timestamp;
        modifiedAt  : Timestamp;
}

               

  • Expose the Model via CAP Service:

If you have service.cds under srv/ folder, then update it with the following code snippet. If you do not have service.cds, then create one.

using com.mycompany.capapp from '../db/schema';

service CatalogService {
    entity Products as projection on capapp.Products;
    }

CAP automatically exposes this service as an OData endpoint without requiring manual REST configuration.

 

  • Add Data:

Run the following in the project terminal:

cds add data --records 5

 

  • Run the Application locally:

Run the following command:

cds watch

You should be able to see the data in the Products

You can also use the SQLite as a database while running locally. Execute these before starting the application:

cds build
cds deploy

 

  • Add a Managed Approuter:
  1. Right click on mta.yaml
  2. Click on Create MTA Module from Template
  3. Select Approuter Configuration
  4. Fill in the required details
  5. Click Finish

 

This is important to view the data once deployed.

 

Prepare the Project for Deployment:

Since we have created the project from the BAS Template, we already have the mta.yaml updated and deployment ready. This file already includes:

  • CAP Service Modules
  • SAP HANA HDI Container
  • Required Service Bindings

 

Build the CAP Project:

First things first, install node modules. Run the following in the Terminal:

npm i

To build the project, you can either run the following in the terminal:

mbt build

or can do it manually:

  • Right click on mta.yaml
  • Select Build MTA Project

This will build your project for deployment. Watch-out at the project structure. You might see a new addition to the structure mta_archives which consists the mtar file of the project.

 

Deploy the Application:

To deploy the application to your BTP using Cloud Foundry, you will have to login to Cloud Foundry. Run the following in Terminal:

cf login

This will ask for your subaccount credentials. Provide them and you will be logged in successfully. Remember to map the correct space and org.

Once you are logged in successfully, you are ready to deploy the application. Remember the file generated while building the CAP project, this is the same file that will be deployed.

Run the following command:

cf deploy mta_archives/cap-hana-app_1.0.0.mtar

or, you can just right click on cap-hana-app_1.0.0.mtar under mta_archives folder, and click on Deploy MTA Archive.

Successful Cloud Foundry deployment

 

During deployment:

  • HDI Container is created
  • Database schema is deployed
  • CAP Service is bound to the database automatically

Confirm the Deployed Instances

 

Verify the Deployed Application:

When the deployment is successful, you will get a application route. Copy the same and open it in the new tab of your browser and access the service endpoint: /catalog/Products

If the deployment is successful, you can see the data displayed in a JSON format.

Browser showing the OData service response

 

Conclusion

With that, we have successfully created a minimal SAP CAP application, connected it to SAP HANA Cloud, and deployed it to SAP BTP using the Cloud Foundry runtime. While the application itself is intentionally simple, the deployment pipeline we established here — from schema definition to MTA build to Cloud Foundry deployment — reflects a production-ready workflow that scales well as complexity grows.

The key takeaways from this exercise are that the BAS CAP template significantly reduces boilerplate by auto-generating the mta.yaml with the necessary modules and bindings, the HDI container handles schema deployment seamlessly, and CAP's convention-over-configuration approach means your OData service is ready without any manual REST wiring.

This baseline will serve as the foundation for the rest of this series, where we will explore deploying the same application with different databases, runtimes, and configurations — making it easier to understand the trade-offs of each approach. Stay tuned for the next part!

 

Next Steps

In the upcoming blogs, we will focus on creating the same application using a PostgreSQL, Hyperscaler Option.

entry.title: Automating Major Maintenance Work Requests with SAP Build Process Automation:S4HANA Asset Management
entry.content:
This blog provides a broad outline of how SAP Business Process Automation can help maintenance teams streamline the "Major Maintenance Work" Request Form, including the Sanction Committee Governance approval process.

What is SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) is a low-code, no-code service on the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) that enables users to automate, manage, and optimize business processes and workflows without writing code.

We can start the business process in four ways: using a request form or an API or an Event or a Scheduled Trigger.

 

My Business Use Case:

My maintenance team is preparing for a major equipment overhaul. They must present the business case with the supporting evidence to the Sanction Committee Governance for FY26 budget approval. Once the sanction committee approves the budget, the maintenance team initiates the maintenance order process.

In this blog post, I will outline the process of creating the major maintenance work request form and explain how the approval/rejection workflow operates. (Note: The automatic creation of maintenance order at the end of the approval process will be outlined in my next blog post).

For my business use case in this blog post, the process is triggered when a form is submitted. The process then continues with a combination of steps, such as Approval Forms, Email notifications until it reaches its end.

 

If you want to gain insights into how SAP JOULE skill works, please check out my latest blog posts below.

  • SAP JOULE & ME : My First JOULE Skill in SAP EHSM-Incident Management Without Coding 
  • SAP Joule Skill for Technical Completion of Maintenance Orders in S/4HANA Asset Management 

 

We will first create a business process project using SAP Build Lobby. Then, we will design the business process. Once the business process is completed, we will create and configure forms. After that, we will create and configure approval form, and finally, we will configure the email notification.

Once we complete the setup, the final result looks like this.

Let us first create a project using SAP Build Process Automation.

Business process projects are a collection of skills in SAP Build Process Automation. Projects are part of the internal business processes of a company and are defined based on business scenarios. A project can contain a set of processes, forms, automations and decisions, which are reusable artifacts.

 

Inside a project, we can create a process. We can create a business process version using a combination of artifacts (such as forms and decisions) and process controls (such as branches, conditions, and Mail Notifications).

Let us now add forms to the business process. For Instance let us use "Form" to trigger the business process.

Let us design the form using "Open Editor".

for instance, let us add Input fields with labels.

  • General Information: we can add the dropdown values wherever required, so that the maintenance team can select the desired value while submitting the form.

  • Scope Of Work

  • Cost Estimate : Projected Cost for Materials and Services

Now the trigger form is ready. Let us design the approval process form.

The approval form is used to get faster and easier approvals from the business users. These forms are converted into tasks in an automated workflow which will appear in the My Inbox of the user. We can design and modify without coding.

Let us design the Approval Form in the form builder by dragging and dropping fields into the form editor and configure the field settings. As the L1 Approver does not have to modify the data, let us make the fields "Read Only" for the L1 approver and add a new field to input L1 Approver Comments.

 

Now, let us configure the L1 Approval form and map the fields. We can use bind list wherever required. The subject that we mention in general section will be displayed when this task appears in "My Inbox". And this task will be appeared in the "My Inbox" of the provided "Recipient".

Similarly, let us create an L2 Approver Form. Let us use the option "Duplicate".

Duplicate feature is used to copy artifacts within the business process. The copy feature is used to copy artifacts across the business process. 

Similarly, let us configure the general information and map the input fields.

We have just completed the approval form for the major maintenance work request process.

My organization demands an email notification to be sent to the requestor whenever there is an action, i.e., Approve or Rejection happens on the Major Maintenance Work Request Form.

Let us configure the Header and Subject of the E-Mail for the "Rejection" process.

Now, let us configure the E-Mail Body.

Similarly, let us configure the Header, Subject and Body of the E-Mail Notification for the "Approval" process.

Once the Major Maintenance Work Request Form with two levels of Approvals  is completed, we can release and deploy it in the respective environment.

Now, Let us run the business process...

Business Case Details:

Scope Of Work Details:

Cost Estimate Details:

Now let us submit the form. 

The L1 Approver can now check "My Inbox"  to review the Major Maintenance Work Request Form.

Since the L1 Approver Comments are mandatory for this form, the option "Approve" or "Rejection" will be highlighted once L1 Approver provides the comments.

Once the Major Maintenance Request Form is approved by the L1 Approver, It will be displayed in L2 Approver`s Inbox. Let us approve it and send an email notification to the requestor upon the approval. L1 Approver comments are displayed while L2 Approver approves or rejects the form.

Once the workflow is ended, we can monitor the processes.

The monitoring page collects the events coming from the deployed workflows and automation jobs and present them to the user. These events represents the status of the jobs that have been run, and the business activity of the monitoring events.

For my business use case, we can view the process, workflow status using "Process and Workflow Instances" application.

😊This brings me to the end of process walkthrough on SAP Build Process Automation using forms as a trigger for one of the use cases, i.e., Major Maintenance Work Request.

In Summary,  we have designed and configured a digital form to capture structured data for a "Major Maintenance Work". Implemented multi level approvals (L1- L2). And also triggered automated email notifications to the requestor, improving transparency and reducing manual efforts. This entire approach established a foundation that can be scaled up and showed how automation can reduce repetitive tasks and speeds up the approval cycle with more transparency.

Stay tuned for more updates on SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Joule Skills/Agents in S/4HANA Asset Management Space...

-- 

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. C

entry.title: Re: Reimagining Certification for the Way We Work Today
entry.content:
Hello @DianaRoesner ,

Yesterday, I took the Employee Central Core and Position Management exam.
I scored 75%, but after clicking "submit," I was redirected to a strange page that told me not to close the page and also included the AI ​​privacy policy. Then the page changed and the text changed to: It looks like you don't have the necessary permissions to view this content. Please make sure you're logged in with your SAP credentials or try switching accounts.


The blue exam bar remained at the top of the page, as if the exam was still active. This wasn't deducted from my subscription either.

Later, it turned out there was a problem and I couldn't take any of the exams.

If the team is working on the website, it's worth letting us know in advance.

I've opened a ticket and am waiting for a response. I hope the result has been saved and I won't have to take the exam again.

entry.title: Re: Rework Production order, its component level price di...
entry.content:
1. Create rework order (CO07) with dismantling BOM.
  2. Issue purchased product (MAP) → order collects cost.
  3. Receive components (531) → order still holds cost.
  4. Settlement (KO88) → distribute cost to components based on BOM percentages.
  5. Components now carry their share of the dismantled product’s MAP, ready for sale.

entry.title: Blog 2 - Accelerating SAP BW Modernization: Hands on Experiences with the BDC Data Product Generator
entry.content:
We explored in part 1 of this blog series the upcoming 2027 End of Maintenance deadline for NW BW 7.5 customers and what they can do next, based on practical experiences in a recent pilot project. This follow-on blog explores once the BW system is ‘lifted’ into SAP Business Data Cloud PCE, thereby extending the maintenance of the as-is environment) what enhanced capabilities there are for customers to kick start their system modernization.

As a recap, the Lift phase in BW to BDC refers to customers migrating their existing SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA on-premises deployment as-is to the private cloud component of SAP Business Data Cloud, without requiring system redesign or data model changes.

Once a customer’s as-is BW is available in PCE as a part of BDC, the steps to modernize can begin. It is well documented that SAP BDC lays the foundation of the Data Product economy for the modern SAP Business Suite, but BDC also is responsible for Data Products created from SAP BW data. The creation of Data Products from BW is orchestrated via the ‘Data Product Generator’ which can be installed on BW once it is available as a part of the BDC formation in PCE. This is applicable for BW7.5 on HANA and/or BW/4HANA.

The BW Data Product Generator (BW DPG) is a tool that enables SAP BW customers to easily replicate InfoProvider data to the managed object store (HDLFS) of SAP Datasphere within SAP Business Data Cloud. It transforms traditional BW data into modern data products for consumption in analytical scenarios and AI/ML workflows. An informative introductory blog on the DPG can be found here

For our complex BW pilot case, we used the BW DPG to generate data products out of potentially decades old data sets, making them available in SAP Datasphere for mixed modelling scenarios, plus via zero copy to SAP Databricks.

In the SAP Gui using transaction code RSDPS, the Data Product Generator lists the subscriptions both active and inactive.

The Data Product Generator for users on the Eclipse modelling environment is also available

A BW DPG subscription is a configuration object in the SAP BW Data Product Generator that defines how data from specific BW InfoProviders should be replicated to SAP Datasphere’s Object Store within SAP Business Data Cloud. It serves as a replication package that specifies which InfoProviders to extract, what data fields to include, and how the replication should be executed (Initial only or Initial with Delta).

Our experience with the data product generator in the pilot project showcased that even in v1 it has great capabilities to access any InfoProvider in SAP BW7.5 and load into the BDC custom data products store in HDLFS. There were optimizations to be made on the time to activate a subscription, and many enhancements have just been delivered, plus more are on the way in the near term roadmap for the DPG.

Screenshot of v2 of the Data Product Generator 

Once the Data Products were available in the BW space in Datasphere, it was very simple to drag and drop them into the canvas for building data models/analytical data sets. Enhancements around association handling (mentioned below) will maintain relationships from the source objects, enabling faster and more complete modelling of the BW Data Products in SAP BDC Datasphere.

 Of course, these data products are available at this stage to be Zero Copy Data shared to SAP Databricks or via BDC Connect to certified targets.

 

Data Product Generator Roadmap

Certain features were not available at the time of our pilot, but are now released, that would have made a big impact on the customer use case. Important items amongst others are mass object handling and semantic onboarding of association information from BW objects.

  • Handling of multiple subscriptions within one tooling (eclipse-based) when selecting an entity from SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW)
  • Inclusion of association information (semantic onboarding)
  • Creation of corresponding subscriptions for dependent entities of the selected entity from SAP BW

These capabilities now being released will enable customers to easily select objects and handle through the creation of multiple subscriptions in one tooling, including information on associations where subscriptions can also be automatically created if selected. This will increase the time to value and enhance the experience for self service modelling in Datasphere, with mappings between the BW objects available automatically.

 

The roadmap information for DPG and other topics can be found here –

https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=FIRST-LAST&BC=6DF54C88F6211FD082B1933C9215B4BE#Q4%202025;INNO=000D3AA914A11FD090BB913F68EFC141

In summary, the Data Product Generator is handling the ‘shift’ phase of the BW modernization journey, creating data products in the HDLFS layer of BDC out of BW Info Providers, making this treasure chest of historic data (plus potentially new data changes) available for modern AI/ML and GenAI use cases.

Stay tuned to our blog series to discover more about how to transition from BW 7.5 to SAP Business Data Cloud whilst reusing Extractors Custom Logic and Models

entry.title: DI API – Unable to Map Project Stage ID in Sales Order
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are using SAP Business One version 10.0 and are currently working on add-on development.

We have developed a add-on loading data into a Sales Order from Project screen. Most of the fields are loading correctly; however, we are facing issues while mapping the Stage field in the Sales Order → Details tab.

Specifically:

  • When a Project is selected, the Stage field in the Sales Order UI is dynamically populated.

  • From an SDK perspective, we would like to understand:

    • How the Stage field is populated based on the selected Project

    • Which DI API / UI API objects, tables, or events are responsible for this dynamic mapping

We kindly request your guidance on:

  • The correct DI API property or approach to map the Project Stage (Stage ID) in the Sales Order.

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Establish an automated continuous end-to-end development process on SAP Business Technology Plat
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Hi experts, I have a below scenario about posting unp...
entry.content: Thank you sir for your feedback. If I modify the order quantity (by subtracting scrap quantity) then it would also impact my PMR component requirements which was already triggered and it would also cause differences in order quantity and BOM requirements quantity interms of reporting, that was my initial concerns. Do you think it would be a better idea to update the SCRAP_QUANTITY in BAPI_PRODORD_CHANGE would be a considerable option ? Problem with my client is they create an order for 20 pieces and if any repair or rework happens in production line then they keep the WIP assembly in quarantine and keep it for months. Only in MES system there will be a visibility on current status of these orders. But if orders are kept as WIP then MRP is not generating new planned orders.. but in reality these materials will be scrapped after many months..

entry.title: Blank National ID value flow from recruiting to onboarding
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We came across this KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3048929/E that says 

  • It will be necessary to Run script to enable "isSkipOnMissingMandatoryInputEntityEnabled".
  • Once we enable this sysconfig value, the nationalId from Recruiting will be skipped if nationalId value is passed as "NULL" or empty string

Has anyone tested this and confirmed that it is working?

Currently, we only send one country's National ID from RCM to ONB.

The other countries' national ID have been disabled in the manage business configuration page. However, some candidates can be from a different country, which we will collect the Passport Number instead of the national ID and passport number will be mapped to a different custom field in Onboarding module. 

We have asked SAP to run the script and performed testing to try sending a NULL value of national ID from RCM to ONB. However, the error message below still appears "

An error occurred while saving the HRIS data for the user June Phing due to the following reason: {"responsePayload":[{"userSysId":"1000000021","hrisSaveStatus":[{"elementType":null,"key":null,"statusCode":"ERROR","errorInfo":"National ID cannot be Empty."}]}]}."
 
@Gopinath_Murugaiyanwould appreciate if could you advise if the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3048929/E should work as what my requirement here is? Thank you.

entry.title: What’s New in Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 - FP01
entry.content:
Today the latest Feature Package 01 (FP01) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 OP was released.

With this feature package we deliver once again great innovations that our customers have been waiting for.

Let's start with two features in the context of TM-based scheduling of ERP documents.
As you certainly know, we have introduced scheduling with TM for Sales Orders with Feature Package 01 of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.
We received a lot of customer feedback on how to improve the functionality especially regarding the selection of means of transport and vehicle type.
Traditionally, TM-based Sales Order Scheduling relied only on a default means of transport. Many customers needed a rule-based, flexible way to determine the correct transport mode (e.g., parcel express, LTL, FTL, truck types) during scheduling of sales orders. Until now, that wasn’t possible.
That's why we introduce the

Rule-Based Means of Transport (MTR) & Vehicle Type Determination for TM-based sales order scheduling

To make TM-based sales order scheduling more flexible we have introduced a  new TM condition type (MTRVT_DET) that enables rule-based determination of Means of Transport and the Vehicle Type during scheduling in TM.
The solution uses the well proven and flexible BRF+ condition technique. 
The rules can evaluate multiple factors such as weight, volume, locations, service levels, transportation groups, material freight groups, and even date validity. They can be maintained across regions or business units, tested via simulation, and exported or imported through Excel.

 

RACR-Screen shows that Means of transport is considered during TRS

Another improvement of this innovation is, that TRS can calculate more accurate lead times as also the appropriate factory calendars, shift sequences, loading and unloading durations, and driving and working constraints are taken into account.
Existing routing methods - including lane-based routing, straight-line distances, default routes, and network path determination - have all been enhanced to respect the selected MTR when it is available.

To support configuration and testing, SAP has also expanded the TRS and Network Path Determination test cockpits with new input parameters and clearer visibility into determination results. Additionally, new BAdIs and append structures allow customers to introduce custom fields or override standard logic while still adhering to clean-core principles.

The big advantage of this great innovation is, that scheduling for sales orders with TRS can now much better reflect real world conditions and therefor provide a much more accurate result to sales representatives. 

Planning and Network

functionality has also been greatly improved and many cool innovations such as

Capacity Document Creation with Load Consolidation

With this enhancement is now possible to combine requirements grouping with load consolidation. The users can dynamically choose between multiple equipment types and use either capacity-based or load-planning-based consolidation.
This new feature improves planning accuracy and reduces manual work and is valid for single-source, single-destination scenarios.

 Creation of capacity document & load consolidation based on freight group

There are also enhancements done in Freight Unit Building:
First of all Packaging information created in Sales Orders and Deliveries is now carried over into Freight Units. As a consequence if packaging already exists, TM will not re-run UPB. Also the system stores the UPB engine and rules used in earlier documents. And with that in place improves integration consistency and transparency.

There are additional enhancements in the Package Building Optimizer (PBO).

We have introduced a Cost-Based Selection of Packaging Materials and with this the package building optimizer can now select the optimal set of cartons/pallets based on fixed costs. This extension supports multi-level packaging (cartons → pallets) and enables mixing of pre-defined packaging and optimized packaging. It also considers dimensional checks, incompatibilities, and capacity.

 Cost-based selection chooses different packaging materials on both packaging levels

The Package Building Optimization now can use an Automatic Runtime Regulation. This means four runtime modes are introduces, so that either the Fastest, Fast, Balanced (recommended) or Quality mode can be chosen. With this  configuration  is simplified and helps balance solution quality vs. speed.

In the area of Load Planning & Load Consolidation also quite some improvements are delivered with this feature package.

The free positioning load planning now also supports mixed pallet footprints, Cylindrical objects and heterogeneous sizes.
This enhancements lead to better container/truck utilization and therefore can help to reduce the number of containers or trucks needed. 
Furthermore a new rectangular selection feature enables quick multi-object selection and drag-and-drop stacking.

Similar like with the PBO it's now also possible to use an automated runtime regulation.

In the area of master data for the transportation network the schedules are enhanced in a way, that now warehouse doors and stops for loading or unloading can be added in the schedules. These stops are taken over to freight orders that are created manually, VSR or generation reports.
By that manually adding of warehouse stops is not needed anymore.

Also it is now possible to add the vehicle type and equipment type to the schedule, which is also carried over to the freight orders.

Schedules extended with vehicle types

Sales Scheduling Agreements

There is also an innovation in the area of scheduling agreements delivered, which has positive influence on performance and system load. Sales Scheduling Agreements accumulate many schedule lines because deliveries occur several times per week or even daily. Since schedule lines in SSAs are deleted once fully delivered, TM had to evaluate all remaining lines with every integration. 
The new enhancement introduces a rule-based, dynamic reduction of the schedule lines considered during TM integration. The concept was adapted from the already existing approach used for Purchase Scheduling Agreements.

The reduction of evaluated schedule line is done by defining rules that determine how many schedule lines are counted within a certain time frame. Depending on this count, the system calculates an integration horizon. That means that the higher the schedule-line density is, the shorter the integration horizon — meaning fewer lines must be processed.

 

Enhancements in Logistics Integration

Feature Package 01 for SAP Transportation Management brings a series of meaningful advancements that strengthen the integration between logistics execution and transportation processes.

The main topics are

  • Ad-hoc loading
  • Discrepancies in the context of Adv. SR and Stock Transport Orders (STO)
  • Adv. SR & warehouse-driven ODO-split
  • Taking over package information from LE outbound
  • Central Update of the Logistics Integration Engine for TM-LE
  • Clean core enhancements

Lets have a look into the topics individually:

Ad-hoc loading

A major highlight is the new ad hoc loading capability, which allows warehouse workers to load goods onto a truck even before freight units are assigned to a freight order. Instead of relying on pre-planned transportation structures, the system now identifies the correct freight order dynamically once a handling unit is scanned, dramatically increasing flexibility for warehouse-driven processes. 

This enhancement in Advanced Shipping & Receiving (Adv. SR) is available for both embedded and decentralized ASR setups. Traditionally, outbound processing required all items in an EWM outbound delivery order to be fully picked or packed before marking the document Ready for Shipping, updating the freight order, and enabling loading. An alternative, Immediate Loading, allows loading to begin as soon as at least one handling unit (HU) is prepared, enabling a streaming flow of goods to the truck.

Ad-hoc loading with RF scanning for unplanned freight 

Ad Hoc Loading adds a third, more flexible approach. It supports lean, warehouse-driven outbound processes without upfront transportation planning. Freight orders may start nearly empty—defined only with a destination—and loading is executed directly via RF. When an HU is scanned, the system checks whether it matches the freight order (e.g., based on ship-to-party). If matched, the HU and corresponding outbound order are dynamically assigned to the freight order during loading. This allows routine, schedule-based transports (e.g., “truck arrives every Wednesday at door 15”) and enables incremental loading of all HUs associated with the scanned document. The feature works primarily through RF transactions (loading by door, by HU, or by bill of lading), offering real-time assignment and visibility of remaining items to load.

Discrepancies for Stock Transport Orders (STO) in the context of Adv. SR

The two step process for STOs in the context of Adv. SR was already introduced with FP0, which means in the process the FU will be re-assigned from the outbound to the inbound document for receiving the goods on the inbound warehouse after the GI was posted on the outbound warehouse.
In the process many discrepancies may occur and they could not be handled so far. Now the process can be reversed by reversing process steps dependent on the execution. For instance if the truck is not departed yet then the inbound delivery can be deleted manually (e.g. in the POWL for FUs) and the FU is reassigned to the outbound freight order again.

 Discrepancies during the Adv. S&R process for STOs

So dependent on the status of process chain execution (transport not departed,;Truck on route, but not received; Truck arrived, and ready for warehouse processing in inbound is set) the various process steps can be reversed manually.
This can be done by deletion of inbound delivery, reversal of goods issue, reversal of warehouse ready for unloading.
Most of the processes are done on the outbound process.
If actions are already done in the inbound receiving process (e.g. putaway actions are done), then the reversals are done on the inbound side of the process.

Adv. SR & Warehouse-driven ODO-split

There may be two cases that requires an ODO (Outbound delivery Order) split in EWM. The first case would be that not all HUs can be planned on a single freight order. In this context a new FU as well as new delivery in EWM is created for the not planned HU.
The second case is that after loading not all HUs of a delivery fit physically to a freight order. Also in this case a new FU and a new delivery is created for the not loaded HUs (after loading is reported in the freight order for the loading point).

4.  Handing over packaging information from LE outbound delivery to TM
In a delivery based scenario where FUs are created based on the delivery, if packaging is automatically done based on the packaging instructions in the outbound delivery, the packaging proposal HU is created in the delivery. This information is now transferred to TM including the LE packaging  building engine and rule. 

 Handover of packaging instructions to TM

 

Logistics Integration Engine for TM-LE for Outbound

A central update is the new Logistics Integration (LI) Engine, which changes how updates are processed once a delivery reaches specific warehouse or transport statuses. Before “ready for warehouse processing” (transport-driven) or before “warehouse processing” (warehouse-driven), users can still freely change key delivery fields. After these statuses, changes to critical fields—such as Incoterms, delivery dates, shipping types, and delivery quantities—are restricted to avoid inconsistencies or unintended freight-unit changes. More information can be found in SAP note 3637618

Clean Core Enhancements

There are two new Badi's.
The first BAdI is to change TRQ Data (LE and EWM). If customers are using the TTRQ BAdI SCMTMS/BADI_LOGINT_CHG_TTRQ already, they also need to implement this new BAdI /SCMTMS/BADI_LI_UH_MAP as the old BAdI is not called anymore after delivery is released (see impact note SAP note 3637618) 

The second new BAdI /SCMTMS/BADI_LI_ERROR_HNDL is to adoption the error handling and enable customer specific Logistics Integration error categories.


Innovations in Freight Order Management

This feature package introduces a series of powerful enhancements to Freight Order Management, aiming to streamline warehouse operations, simplify Adv. SR (Advanced Shipping & Receiving), and strengthen collaboration with carriers. 

An important innovation is the enablement of Adv. SR without warehouse master data. Previously, customers using simplified shipping scenarios were forced to maintain minimal warehouse configurations just to activate Adv. SR. With the new release, warehouse loading stops can be determined without any warehouse setup, lowering the entry barrier for businesses with lightweight logistics processes and reducing unnecessary configuration effort.

 Adv. SR without warehouse information creates warehouse loading stops

We also enhanced the status part of the Adv. SR process, offering more automation and removing manual steps. Users can now define rule-based item status updates for loading and unloading, allowing the system to automatically mark relevant packages as loaded or unloaded. Additionally, warehouse loading stops can now be automatically set to completion once certain events occur—while still retaining safeguards to avoid unintended follow-up processes. These features together provide a more flexible and efficient execution flow for diverse warehouse environments.

Enhancements to Web Service TransportationOrderConfirmation_In

In the area of external planning, two major enhancements stand out. First, carriers can now perform freight unit splits—for example, when only part of a shipment fits on a given truck. The system accepts partial planning results, creates the appropriate split freight units and deliveries, and seamlessly updates the freight order. This gives carriers more realistic flexibility, especially in industries with varying cargo sizes, and removes restrictions that previously required perfect one-to-one planning.

Second, external planning now supports driver information exchange, enabling carriers to transmit driver details directly through the transportation order interface. This allows the shipper to store and use this information immediately during check-in and execution without manual updates.

We have enhanced the TransportationOrderConfirmation_In web service, to enabling richer and more flexible driver data updates for freight orders. Previously, the system allowed only two drivers to be assigned and lacked the ability to map drivers to individual transportation stages. The new driver node overcomes these limitations by supporting multi-driver, multi-stage assignments while reducing dependency on manual address entry.

Carriers can now assign any number of drivers and specify exactly which stages they belong to, enabling accurate and scalable handling of complex, multi-stage freight scenarios. When a driver already exists as a business partner in the shipper’s system, carriers can simply provide the internal ID without needing to resend address information; the system automatically retrieves and updates the driver’s profile.

The release also includes supporting content for the ongoing migration from legacy event-handling logic to the new Execution Information Framework. SAP provides detailed documentation, compatibility checks, and a comprehensive cookbook, helping customers move away from deprecated APIs and ensuring their custom logic remains compatible with upcoming changes. Furthermore, the team shared a practical enhancement—developed in collaboration with customer feedback—that automates warehouse-processing status updates for freight units when carriers are assigned, using the Change Controller Framework. This gives customers a template for extending Adv. SR behavior without modifying standard logic

Automatic attachment creation for paperless shipments

There are not always printouts required for freight orders or other execution documents. And often it is desired to not only print a pdf document, but also automatically store the pdf document as an attachment for instance in the freight order or freight booking.

Now Output (print) documents from PPF in TM can automatically asynchronically be stored as pdf attachment in the execution document. This functionality is customizable and can be turned on in the freight unit, freight order, freight booking, transportation unit, consignment order based on the type customizing.

Enabling Charge Hierarchy in Ocean Tendering

The Ocean Tendering process has been enhanced with a more transparent and flexible pricing model. Previously, shippers and carriers communicated using a simple price limit—a single value exchanged during tendering. While functional, this one-line approach lacked cost breakdowns and made it difficult for shippers to understand what they were paying for or for carriers to negotiate effectively.

To address this, the new release introduces two extended pricing options: Charge Hierarchy and Price Limit to Charge Hierarchy. These offer detailed cost structures that help both parties understand charges such as loading, unloading, port fees, base rates, and other surcharges.

With Charge Hierarchy, shippers send a complete cost breakdown through EDI, and carriers must respond using the same structure. This brings granular transparency and supports cleaner negotiations. The system also uses configuration settings to determine whether charge details are included in the EDI messages.

 Charge hierarchy in ocean booking

The Price Limit to Charge Hierarchy option takes a hybrid approach. Shippers continue to send a simple price limit but signal that they expect the carrier to reply with a full charge breakdown. This provides flexibility for carriers to distribute the total cost into detailed components while keeping the shipper’s initial request simple.

 

Collective Settlement Process with Lean Service Procurement in TM

We introduce the ability to perform collective settlement posting for Lean Service Procurement.
Previously, Lean freight documents could only be posted individually, but the new release finally makes it possible to group multiple freight documents into a single settlement document.

To support this we enhanced two key Fiori apps: Monitor Freight Documents for Accrual and Monitor Freight Settlements for Accrual. Users can now select multiple freight documents and choose whether they should be posted individually, collectively, or according to preset profile rules.

In the process of collective settlement failed collective posting may happen. In such a case the documents are automatically move to the error tab, where users can fix issues and reattempt collective settlement.

Once a collective settlement is created, the system allows users to see all freight documents associated with it directly from the settlement screen. This visibility addresses a long-standing request to make collective groupings fully transparent. The batch report has also been upgraded to support collective postings for Lean Service Procurement documents, while continuing to honor split criteria such as purchasing group, carrier, or Lean/non-Lean mixing—ensuring that incompatible combinations are automatically separated into different settlement documents.

 

 Collective Settlement from App for Accruals and  Carrier Invoice

There may be two change scenarios in the context of Lean Service Order settlement.
First: When a new charge is added to an already posted freight order, a delta settlement is created without disturbing the existing collective settlement. Second: When an existing charge is changed, the system must cancel the original collective settlement entirely. Once the cancellation occurs, the involved freight orders revert to a state where they can be reposted—some returning to “ready for confirmation,” others to “ready for posting,” depending on what was modified.

New charges can still be added even after a collective settlement has been invoice-verified,- However, existing charge items cannot be modified once verified. 

The Invoice verification process is another highlight.
A new search help field now allows users to retrieve all freight orders linked to a collective settlement simply by entering its settlement number. Users may also invoice individual freight orders from a collective settlement if needed.
Once invoiced, all related freight documents reflect the invoice-verified status, and a single carrier invoice is created if the posting is done collectively.

The freight cost allocation document—generated after settlement—is now structured so that each line clearly corresponds to an individual freight document. Depending on configuration, mappings can result in either one item per freight document or multiple items when multiple condition types are used.

Charge Calculation at consignment order level

A significant functional expansion is the introduction of cost distribution for consignment orders. Until now, this capability existed only for freight orders. With the new release, consignment orders can calculate freight charges, distribute them across deliveries, and even post them directly into financial accounting. This creates a consistent cost-handling process for businesses that rely primarily on consignment orders and eliminates duplicate or parallel processing steps. The integration ensures that distributed costs flow cleanly into ERP documents, maintaining profit center assignments and financial transparency

SAP Transportation Management now supports charge calculation and settlement at the consignment level, enabling more accurate cost allocation when multiple consignments are combined within a freight order. A consignment order groups transportation requirements such as freight units and products tied to specific pickup and delivery locations. The update allows freight orders to calculate charges individually per consignment or in consolidated form when consignments share the same supplier and source location. This is achieved through a new consignment resolution base, which can be assigned within calculation sheets alongside existing bases like route or product.

During charge calculation, the system can either compute charges separately for each consignment or merge them using charge type grouping to produce consolidated rate lines. Settlement behavior remains unchanged, with all calculated charges carried forward into the settlement document. Cost distribution has been enhanced to ensure charges linked to consignment-based resolution are distributed only to products that belong to the corresponding consignment. This applies to both direct and hierarchical distribution, producing accurate allocation documents and correct posting to expense or material accounts. Demonstrations showed both non-grouped and grouped scenarios, confirming consistent charge calculation, distribution logic, and settlement flow.

 

AI-Assisted Goods Receipt Analysis with Bordero Documents

SAP’s AI-assisted Goods Receipt Analysis continues to evolve, expanding capabilities first introduced in 2023. Originally focused on extracting data from delivery notes and shipping documents to create consignment orders, the solution now adds support for the more complex bordero document—critical for gaining a full picture of incoming truckloads when EDI messages are missing or incomplete.

 Transfer of printed information to TM execution document using AI

In many real-world scenarios, trucks arrive at the gate before any system documents exist. The new bordero-based process solves this gap by allowing users to upload scanned carrier documents, which SAP Doc Services then classify and extract using AI. The system identifies carrier details, embedded shipping documents or delivery notes, and all cargo items, mapping them directly to freight orders, consignments, and product items in Transportation Management. This provides immediate visibility into what the truck is carrying and enables downstream processing such as check-in, warehouse handling, and goods receipt.

The S/4HANA 2025 FP01 update also introduces improved error handling. Users can now resolve common extraction issues—such as missing bordero IDs, undetermined suppliers, or mismatched shipping documents—through guided correction actions. A new drag-and-drop upload interface further speeds up document submission across consignment orders and freight orders.

Together, these enhancements significantly reduce manual effort, increase processing accuracy, and shorten turnaround time at the gate and in the warehouse. The result is a more resilient inbound logistics process, even when digital integration with suppliers and carriers is incomplete.


These are the main innovations in TM for FP01.
FP02 development is ongoing.
If you are curious about what will be delivered, check the items published on the Roadmap Explorer already now.

We have also scheduled  some enablement sessions for TM and the features delivered with FP01.
You can register with the following link to the sessions: Supply Chain Management webinars 

For more information, please visit the following sections:

  • What’s New Viewer
  • SAP TM Product Page on sap.com
  • SAP Community for Transportation Management
  • SAP Transportation Management Group on LinkedIn

entry.title: Re: Bank statement: Ignore partner information for batch...
entry.content:
Good day @FabianSCC 

Thank you for your question.

Your observation is valid. When ns1:NbOfTxs contains a recurring value for a batch posting, the parser still has detail records and the mapping node (for example Set Creditor IBAN), can end up using the last detail transaction for the whole batch line. This causes the partner’s name or partner bank account to be misleading for batch entries. See the following references on format mapping capabilities in the Public Cloud for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/5481c611480e4c23bf49e3af651d8e4d.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e2d057b7b4df44ba941a040d4dda2956/3d533a91dff746b19e692959c0c2f3d5.html?locale=en-US

Question: Is it possible to add another condition? Or do you see any other possibility to solve this issue?

Answer: Usually yes. It is recommended to make the node condition batch aware. If your node already has a condition, you typically should:

  1. Extend the existing condition definition (combine with AND/OR), or
  2. You split the logic into two nodes/action with exclusive conditions that are mutual.

For your case, I would recommend updating both. Set Creditor IBAN and set Credito Name or its equivalent node. So, they run when NbOfTxs = 1. That will prevent the batch entry from inheriting the last payment’s partner details.

You may consider the following steps:

  • Using the Map Format Data for Incoming Files from Banks app, copy CAMT.053 mapping into your customer namespace.

  • Open the mapping tree and identify the partner name/IBAN nodes.
  • Adjust the condition definition to include ns1:NbOfTxs = 1 (or you may create a separate branch for <>1).
  • You can then test with one normal single transaction and one batch transaction.
  • Then import with your custom mapping and verify the result in bank statement processing.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e2d057b7b4df44ba941a040d4dda2956/3d533a91dff746b19e692959c0c2f3d5.html?locale=en-US

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2602/SolP/16R?region=DE

To summarise:

  • Yes, your idea is correct to use ns1:NbOfTxs to prevent partner data mapping for batch entries.
  • Extend or split your nodes into two if one has already a condition.
  • Use the same logic to both partner IBAN and partner name nodes.
  • Use your customer mapping to test with one single and one batch statement file.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Beta Release of SAP Signavio Transformation Manager Objectives API
entry.content:
After the successful Beta launch of the Initiatives and Insights APIs , we are happy to announce the next addition to the SAP Signavio Transformation Manager API family: the Objectives API. 

With this launch, Objectives, the strategic foundation of your transformation initiatives, are now programmatically accessible. The Objectives API allows you to integrate your strategic objectives directly into your enterprise landscape and link them to initiatives, insights, and performance data. 

This is another important step towards SAP Signavio Transformation Manager becoming not only a cockpit for execution but also a fully integrated system for strategy-to-value orchestration. 

Why is the Objectives API important? 

In a typical transformation landscape, strategy and execution are decoupled: 

  • Objectives are maintained in strategy decks or portfolio management solutions. 
  • Initiatives are managed in project management systems.
  • Operational data is stored in analytics platforms. 
  • Governance is managed in other systems. 

This leads to a lack of integration. Alignment is done by hand. Reporting is inconsistent. 

The Objectives API fills this gap by allowing you to: 

  1. Strategically Integrate Across Systems 

You can now fetch and synchronize objectives from SAP Signavio directly into your enterprise applications using the REST API. 

This enables you to: 

  • Integrate transformation objectives into enterprise reporting. 
  • Synchronize portfolio dashboards with strategic objectives. 
  • Use consistent objective naming and metadata across systems. 
  1. Provide Clear Strategy-to-Execution Traceability 
  • The API allows you to fetch: 
  • A particular objective by its Suite Objective ID. 
  • The initiatives associated with a given objective. 

This establishes a direct, machine-readable connection between: 

  • Strategic goals (Objectives) 
  • Execution vehicles (Initiatives) 
  • Operational improvement signals (Insights) 

For transformation leaders, this means traceable value from strategy definition to value realization. 

  1. Standardized, Secure Access 
  • The Objectives API is consistent with the security framework of other Transformation Manager APIs: 
  • Standardized response formats 
  • Bearer authentication 

What’s Included in the Beta 

The Objectives API adds the following endpoint under: 

/transformationmanager/v1/objectives 

List Objectives 

Fetch, search and filter objectives with pagination support. 

Important parameters: 

  • page (zero-based index) 
  • size (max 1000) 
  • $count=true (returns total count) 

Response includes: 

  • Objective ID 
  • Name and description 
  • Creation and update timestamps 
  • Additional attributes (raw JSON): 
    • dateCreated 
    • dateUpdated 
    • name 
    • description 
    • attributes 
    • Etc. 

Get Objective by SID 

Fetch a specific objective by its Suite Objective ID: 

GET /objectives/by-sid/{objectiveSid} 

Get Initiatives for an Objective 

Fetch initiatives associated with a given objective: 

GET /objectives/by-sid/{objectiveSid}/initiatives 

This endpoint enables strategy-to-execution traceability by returning initiative summaries including: 

  • Status 
  • Value drivers 
  • Ownership and authorization data 
  • Timeline data 

Check this blog to learn more about Initiatives API.  

Example Integration Scenarios 

Enterprise Reporting Integration 

Synchronize objectives into: 

  • SAP Analytics Cloud dashboards 
  • Power BI reporting layers 
  • Executive portfolio dashboards 

This aligns strategic KPIs and transformation objectives on the same reporting model. 

Integrate Transformation Manager Objectives with OKR platforms (e.g., Tangible Growth): 

  • Synchronize strategic objectives between Tangible Growth and SAP Signavio Transformation Manager 
  • Map OKRs to linked objectives for execution traceability 
  • Reflect initiative status and progress back into OKR dashboards 
  • Maintain a consistent strategic reference model across systems 

This connects strategy definition (OKRs) with execution tracking (initiatives and insights), enabling closed-loop governance between planning and transformation delivery. 

Integrate Transformation Manager Objectives with OKR platforms (e.g., Tangible Growth): 

  • Synchronize strategic objectives between Tangible Growth and SAP Signavio Transformation Manager 
  • Map OKRs to linked objectives for execution traceability 
  • Reflect initiative status and progress back into OKR dashboards 
  • Maintain a consistent strategic reference model across systems 

This connects strategy definition (OKRs) with execution tracking (initiatives and insights), enabling closed-loop governance between planning and transformation delivery. 

Automation & Governance 

Automatically: 

  • Check if initiatives are connected to strategic objectives. 
  • Identify orphan initiatives. 
  • Launch workflows based on objective updates. 

AI and Advisory Scenarios 

Together with: 

  • Initiatives API 
  • Insights API 
  • AI Transformation Advisor functionality 

You can programmatically assess: 

  • Which objectives are under-supported. 
  • Which value drivers are over-represented. 
  • Where execution gaps are found. 

This enables data-driven portfolio management at scale. 

Who Should Explore the Objectives API 

  • Enterprise architects integrating strategy and execution tiers. 
  • Transformation leaders implementing portfolio governance. 
  • Developers implementing automation or AI use cases on SAP Signavio. 
  • Customers integrating Transformation Manager into their enterprise reporting infrastructure. 

If your company is already using the Initiatives and Insights APIs, the Objectives API rounds out the strategy-to-execution workflow. 

How to Get Started 

  1. Find the API documentation on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. 
  2. Explore the Objectives API endpoints and authentication configuration on SAP Help. 
  3. Test integration use cases in your sandbox environment. 
  4. Provide feedback to the SAP Signavio product team. 

Your feedback during the Beta phase will inform the General Availability roadmap. 

What’s Next 

After the Beta phase, we intend to: 

  • Enhance the Objectives API functionality. 
  • Intensify integration with value case modeling. 
  • Support more complex cross-asset querying. 
  • Reinforce strategy alignment patterns throughout the Suite.
